briefing.headline: Rig up a Chatterbait — aggressive winter approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Chatterbait is a strong choice for winter with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

KVD: Thunder Cricket in shallow grass even in upper 30s water. Use 3/8 oz (lighter than normal) with straight-tail blade minnow trailer for subtle action. Pull and stop, not steady retrieve. Get it down in old dead vegetation — it holds heat and bass are drawn to it. Tungsten model has smaller blade

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, creek channel, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use a minnow-profile trailer (like CrushCity Freeloader) for natural, erratic movement - it adds realism and bulk without impeding the natural balsa-like hunting action of the ChatterBait.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, creek channel, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: Bass in super cold water are focused on vibration. They use their lateral line to feed — they can feel the presence of shad, the vibration and water displacement of your bait. That's why flatside crankbaits, lipless crankbaits, and bladed jig...
Yamamoto pro Hite: Standard Zako for ChatterBaits (subtle natural action), Kickin' Zako (paddle tail) for deep heavy ChatterBaits on ledges with poor light penetration and for swim jigs exclusively

Wheeler: On highland reservoirs like Cherokee, smallmouth live around holes/depressions (20 ft around the side) — if rock is around them the smallies live there. If a rock vein goes into the middle, they live on those veins. The holes harbor the b...

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal winter bass stage at 12-25ft on main lake structure. Falling pressure before a front is often the best winter fishing window. Fish move slightly shallower and feed more aggressively than normal winter behavior.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If moving baits aren't getting bit, slow down to a crawling retrieve or switch to a jig
